milestone_brief: Implement roadmap epic realtime-variety-render-e5 (plans/realtime-variety-render-roadmap.md section 6.3) -- 'Numba JIT field-evaluation kernel v0', candidate CAND-2. Replace the NumPy-broadcasting scalar-field evaluation for the two highest-cost implicit generators -- Fermat quartic and the Enriques canonical sextic (Fig. 1) -- with @njit(parallel=True, cache=True) Numba kernels, targeting a measured >=5x field-eval speedup visible in the CAND-12 telemetry. Field evaluation is 41-45 percent of total surface.generate() latency. Slice: surfaces.py (two @njit(parallel=True, cache=True) kernel functions replacing the NumPy meshgrid-broadcasting field expressions in the fermat_quartic and enriques_figure_1 generators; set the workqueue Numba threading layer at surfaces.py import time -- numba.config.THREADING_LAYER = 'workqueue' BEFORE any kernel is first called -- so Numba does not contend with VTK's SMP threads inside the e4 worker); requirements.txt (add numba>=0.65,<0.66); tests/ (per-generator numerical spot-check: the njit kernel output must match the NumPy reference to floating-point tolerance at >=3 parameter points each for Fermat and Enriques canonical -- pure NumPy/Numba, no Qt). Spike findings (.claude/notes/roadmaps/realtime-variety-render/spike-numba-arm64.md, verdict VALIDATED-WITH-CAVEAT): (1) the roadmap's numba>=0.60,<0.62 pin is STALE -- numba 0.60-0.61 does not support numpy 2.4.x; the correct pin is numba>=0.65,<0.66 (numba 0.65.1 verified against numpy 2.4.6, no numpy downgrade). (2) First-call JIT latency (~700-1500 ms) EXCEEDS the 500 ms [SHOULD] budget -- do NOT add an eager startup warm-up; instead rely on the e4 background-thread worker absorbing the first-call compile off the GUI thread, and use cache=True so the JIT cost is once-per-machine. The roadmap's 'startup warm-up <=500 ms' acceptance signal is unachievable as written and should be reworded to 'first-call JIT happens off the GUI thread (e4 worker) and cache=True is set'. Hard constraints: AI-1 (no new renderer; numba is a pure compute dependency, allowed); AI-2 (Qt-free tests -- the numerical spot-checks are pure NumPy/Numba, no Qt); AI-6 (implicit pipeline only -- the kernels feed _marching_cubes_to_polydata; the parametric Hanson generators are untouched); AI-8 (generator params are floats; any int coercion stays inside generators); AI-14 (generators still return pv.PolyData or raise ValueError -- the kernel changes only how the field array is computed, not the contract). The kernels must be numerically identical to the current NumPy field expressions -- the existing Fermat quartic and Enriques canonical sextic field formulas in surfaces.py are the reference. Predecessor e4 (background-thread worker) is complete; the Numba arm64 spike passed.
research_synthesis: Both briefs converge: two @njit(parallel=True, cache=True) out-array-fill kernels (prange outer i-loop) replace the NumPy meshgrid-broadcasting in fermat_quartic + enriques_figure_1; np.clip folded into the kernel as scalar min/max for numeric identity. workqueue threading layer set at surfaces.py import top. numba>=0.65,<0.66 (spike-corrected from the stale roadmap pin). cache=True + the e4 worker absorb first-call JIT off the GUI thread -- no eager warm-up. New tests/test_numba_field_kernels.py asserts kernel==NumPy reference at >=3 param points each, tolerance rtol=atol=1e-9 (agent-a -- safe vs op-fusion drift; agent-b said 1e-12). No set_num_threads cap: only one e4 worker runs at a time (single-flight guard) and the Numba kernel + VTK Flying Edges are sequential within generate(), so there is no concurrent oversubscription.
